data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead in an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Do not mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; do not use parallel tool calls for `git add`, `git commit`, or other staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 still allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-04e89823">
    <title>store: cascade-close superseded children inside Close()</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Beads marked superseded-by:<Y> never close automatically when Y closes. ddx-847df529 (parent ddx-09d7d787) carries superseded-by:ddx-c2a66f45-wt-6bee7238 and a note "Do not dispatch this bead independently"; the superseder closed weeks ago but the leftover stays open and blocks its parent epic. This is a primary contributor to the stuck-queue state on the current project (RC-2 in the FEAT-010 reason taxonomy).

ROOT CAUSE
cli/internal/bead/store.go Close(Y) closes only Y. It does not look for open beads X with superseded-by==Y and cascade-close them. FEAT-010 §Idle-Path Diagnosis and Auto-Remediation now requires this cascade with a strict scope guard.

PROPOSED FIX
Extend cli/internal/bead/store.go Close(Y) with a post-close cascade step. After Y closes, scan for open beads X where supersession-by-Y is the sole remaining reason to stay open. Strict scope contract (each must hold; if any fails, surface in ddx work focus Section A, do NOT cascade):
- X is open AND (X.superseded-by == Y OR X carries label superseded-by:Y) AND Y is now closed.
- X has no other open dependents (no open bead Z with Z.dependencies referencing X).
- X has no open dependencies of its own beyond the supersession marker.
- X has attempt_count == 0.
- X has no operator notes added after the supersession marker timestamp.
- X is not currently claimed or in-progress (no live claim_id).
- X carries no manual-hold / no-auto-close / container override label.

Re-read X's current tracker state inside the same Close(Y) invocation before mutating — a concurrent worker may have changed X since the scan started.

Idempotent: re-running Close(Y) is a no-op for already-closed X.
Cycle-guarded: track visited IDs within one Close(Y) call; cascade is one hop deep. If X is a superseder for an open Z, Z's cascade is DEFERRED to a separate operator action (do not transitively cascade).
Audit: emit a superseded_close event on X with body {closed_by_cascade_of: Y, reason: "closed_as_superseded_via:Y"}. Operators can grep events to reverse.

Add operator override flag at the work command surface in a separate bead (--no-auto-supersede-close); the Close() code path always runs the cascade unless the flag has been threaded through. For this bead, accept the cascade always firing; the flag wiring is the work-loop bead's responsibility.

NON-SCOPE
- Cascading transitively beyond one hop (deferred to a future bead if ever needed).
- The work-loop wiring of --no-auto-supersede-close flag (separate bead).
- RC-3 dead-intermediate cleanup walk-up (separate bead, depends on this one).

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b. No deps.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/bead/store.go Close() performs a one-hop supersession cascade post-close, gated by every scope check listed in the description.
2. TestClose_SupersededCascade_Eligible in cli/internal/bead/store_test.go: creates Y (open), X (open, superseded-by:Y, attempt_count==0, no other state), closes Y, asserts X is closed with a superseded_close event whose body cites closed_by_cascade_of: Y.
3. TestClose_SupersededCascade_OperatorNotedNotCascaded: creates Y, X (open, superseded-by:Y, has operator notes added after the supersession marker timestamp), closes Y, asserts X remains open and no superseded_close event was emitted.
4. TestClose_SupersededCascade_OneHopCycleGuard: creates Y, X (open, superseded-by:Y), Z (open, superseded-by:X). Closes Y. Asserts X closes, Z remains open (cascade deferred), no infinite loop.
5. TestClose_SupersededCascade_Idempotent: closing an already-closed Y a second time produces no new events and no mutations.
6. TestClose_SupersededCascade_ClaimedOrInProgress_NotCascaded: X has a live claim_id; closing Y does not cascade X.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... -count=1 green.
8. cd cli && go test ./... green (no regressions in other packages).
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>kind:feat, area:bead-lifecycle, phase:2, spec:FEAT-010, spec:reliability-principles</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-2a1ece2b" base-rev="745a5d2b1637e12d702d732fbc277c637658cfec" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260516T180016-6b66ca90"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read them first. Ground decisions in repository state; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>
